Author: TTBot#6027
Content:
✅ MichaelBell reported @RebelMike's [TinyQV Risc-V SoC](https://www.tinytapeout.com/chips/ttgf0p2/tt_um_MichaelBell_tinyQV) as working: Tested so far:
- Micropython boots OK up to a clock speed of 59MHz(!) although some issues with peripherals at high clock speeds.  Most testing has been at the regular 24MHz.
- Peripherals tested to at least some extent, no issues found so far: Gamepad, PWM, PWL Synth, CORDIC, DWARF, UBCD
- Flash and PSRAM setup from power off state works

Content: Woohoo! Experiment-vill, here I come!

Author: TTBot#6027
Content: ✅ flummer reported @caioalonso's [Register bank accessible through SPI and I2C](https://www.tinytapeout.com/chips/ttgf0p2/tt_um_calonso88_spi_i2c_reg_bank) as working: Tested with Bus Pirate connected to bidir PMOD pins as my homemade demo board doesn't have all bidir pins connected to RP2040. I was able to write to register 0x00 with both SPI and I2C and see result on output pins in commander. Could also write with SPI and read afterwards with I2C. Tried to read all 8 static bytes and using I2C I could do multi read, with SPI I only got the first byte and 0x00 for all the rest in multibyte mode (could be user error), but individually I could read them all

Author: TTBot#6027
Content: ✅ flummer reported 's [Wildcat RISC-V](https://www.tinytapeout.com/chips/ttgf0p2/tt_um_schoeberl_wildcat) as working: I see human readable counting (0 to F) on a 7 segment display on the output PMOD (my PMOD seems to be active low, so the segments are inverted, but the output should match the normal demo board)

Author: TTBot#6027
Content: 🔍 flummer reported @javier's [MarcoPolo](https://www.tinytapeout.com/chips/ttgf0p2/tt_um_javibajocero_top) as partially working: I do get a reply back via UART, but I don't see a change in the LED (could be that I'm not using the exact type, though I have tried a few different and they seem to work when used in another setup and driven as WS2812b). The LED also does not start up being red after a reset.
